title: Add knowledge page with design analysis for GH #106 and #107

comments.author: maxine-at-forecast--noether
comments.content: Researching GH #106 (agent key isolation) and #107 (allowed_signers trust model). Will review current implementation, think through design tradeoffs, and write a knowledge page with findings.

comments.author: maxine-at-forecast--noether
comments.content: Key observation: The two issues are deeply linked. #106 (key isolation) is about preventing agents from impersonating each other at the crypto layer. #107 (trust nomenclature) is about whether the system's naming overpromises on what guarantees are actually provided. Container isolation (PR #150, already in progress) is the natural answer to #106, and it clarifies #107 — when agents are isolated, allowed_signers becomes clearly a 'phonebook' for verifying others' signatures, not an access control list.

comments.author: maxine-at-forecast--noether
comments.content: Delivered: Knowledge page 'signing-trust-design' written with full analysis of GH #106 (agent key isolation) and #107 (allowed_signers trust model). Key recommendations: (1) Container isolation is the right answer for #106, current permissions adequate for non-containerized, (2) Rename allowed_signers → known_signers for #107, (3) Add valid-after timestamps for lifecycle, (4) Don't build crypto-on-crypto integrity protection.
